plan: Identify what reaction "element A + water (high temperature) → binary compound B" constrains about A.
Identify what reaction "B + NaOH + Br₂ → ternary compound C" implies about B and C.
Set up the mass-percent equation using the two-metal 1:1 ratio constraint in C.
Solve for the atomic mass of A and the oxygen count in C.

Identify element A and verify all conditions for compound C. --- ## Solution **Step 1 – Identify the type of reaction for B** At high temperatures, many metals react with steam to give a *binary* metal oxide + H₂: $$2A + 3H_2O \xrightarrow{\Delta} A_2O_3 + 3H_2$$ The binary compound B is therefore the metal oxide of A (two elements only: A and O). **Step 2 – Determine what C must be** C is ternary and contains *two metal elements* in 1:1 ratio. The only metal introduced in the second step is sodium (from NaOH). Hence C contains A and Na in a 1:1 ratio with oxygen → formula **NaAO_n**. **Step 3 – Set up the mass-percent equation** Let M_A = atomic mass of A. $$\frac{M_A}{23 + M_A + 16n} = 0.746$$ $$M_A = 0.746(23 + M_A + 16n)$$ $$0.254\, M_A = 17.16 + 11.94n$$ $$M_A = 67.6 + 47.0n$$ **Step 4 – Solve for n and identify A** | n | M_A | |---|-----| | 1 | 114.6 – no stable element | | 2 | 161.6 – no match | | **3** | **208.6 ≈ 209 → Bismuth (Bi, 208.98)** ✓ | **Step 5 – Verify all conditions for C = NaBiO₃** | Criterion | Check | |-----------|-------| | Two metals in 1:1 ratio | Na : Bi = 1 : 1 ✓ | | Ternary compound | Na, Bi, O ✓ | | No water of crystallisation | anhydrous NaBiO₃ ✓ | | % A by mass | 208.98 / 279.97 = **74.6%** ✓ | | Relative formula mass > 230 | 23 + 209 + 48 = **280** ✓ | | Strong oxidiser | NaBiO₃ is a very powerful oxidant (oxidises Mn²⁺ to MnO₄⁻) ✓ | **Reaction chain confirmed:** $$2\text{Bi} + 3\text{H}_2\text{O} \xrightarrow{\Delta} \underbrace{\text{Bi}_2\text{O}_3}_{B} + 3\text{H}_2$$ $$\text{Bi}_2\text{O}_3 + 6\text{NaOH} + 2\text{Br}_2 \rightarrow 2\underbrace{\text{NaBiO}_3}_{C} + 4\text{NaBr} + 3\text{H}_2\text{O}$$ FINAL ANSWER NaBiO₃

trajectory.thought: At high temperatures, element A reacts with water (steam) to produce a binary compound B. A binary compound contains exactly two elements. Since water provides H and O, and the product is binary (two elements only), the product must contain only A and O (the hydrogen escapes as H₂ gas). This is the classic metal + steam reaction, e.g., 2A + 3H₂O → A₂O₃ + 3H₂, where B = A₂Oₙ is a metal oxide. Element A must therefore be a metal that reacts with steam at high temperatures to form a metal oxide.

The reaction of binary compound B (bismuth oxide, Bi₂O₃) with NaOH and Br₂ yields a ternary compound C. Since Br₂ acts as an oxidant, it oxidizes Bi³⁺ to Bi⁵⁺. The ternary compound C contains two metal elements (Bi and Na) in 1:1 ratio with no water of crystallization, giving NaBiO₃. The reaction is: Bi₂O₃ + 6NaOH + 2Br₂ → 2NaBiO₃ + 4NaBr + 3H₂O.
The plan is already complete and fully worked out. C = NaBiO₃ with formula mass 280 and 74.6% Bi by mass. No tool call needed.
From the equation M_A = 67.6 + 47.0n, testing n=3 gives M_A ≈ 208.6, which matches Bismuth (Bi, 208.98). This confirms A = Bi and C = NaBiO₃ with formula mass = 23 + 209 + 48 = 280 > 230. ✓
